data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 822a "Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode 'expected-bug' guards"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, repo-corroborated against the AS-SHIPPED code. VERDICT: clean pairing — NO G4 cross-child finding; findings list EMPTY.

REPO RE-VERIFICATION (this pass):
- 822a's de-encode work SHIPPED: a search for `verify-inbound-comments-gap` under scripts/jira-pressure-test returns NO matches, and the only remaining `NOT-SYNCED` occurrence is an EXPLANATORY comment at e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:1535 ("…NOT-SYNCED), locking the bug in as 'expected'. De-encoded here so the fix is…"). The encoded-bug guard is gone — converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes per AC2. A search for issuelinks under the probe scripts confirms the prior premise (no probe round-trips relationships).
- 822a's hermetic round-trip SHIPPED: tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/test_roundtrip.py exists (test_roundtrip_converges_links_and_parent L212, test_roundtrip_comments_echo_suppressed_native_synced L249, plus inbound/outbound legs). It sits in the SAME jira_contract/ directory as fe57's _fakes.py/test_jira_fixtures.py, 8937's test_snapshot_contract.py, and 59ef's test_verified_fake_contract.py — DISTINCT files, no co-edit.
- 822a file_impact (test_roundtrip.py [new]; e2e_field_validation_probe.sh [de-encode]; docs/jira-sync-setup.md [live-variant doc]) is DISJOINT from every sibling's edits. 822a declares depends_on fe57 (its only input).

7-MODE WALK (parent × 822a):
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. test_roundtrip.py and the probe script are net-new / solely 822a's; no sibling touches them. The fixtures/fake it consumes are PRODUCED by fe57 — clean producer/consumer split over the declared depends_on fe57 edge; no shared mutable write. The outbound-applier cluster (33d0/2359) and the schema/contract files (8937/59ef) are disjoint.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. 822a AC1 round-trips comments, links, and parent — NOT labels — and asserts inbound↔outbound OUTCOMES, not the outbound `labels_applied` sub-op payload. The epic's one real representation conflict (labels list-vs-int, correctly localized to the 33d0↔2359 outbound-telemetry pairing and resolved as-shipped via integer counters) is therefore untouched by 822a. Its "production path" assumption is consistent with fe57 (unmassaged fixtures through fetch_snapshot), 8937 (snapshot-entry schema), and 33d0 (surviving production batch applier).
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 822a's only input dependency — story A's fixtures/fake — is correctly declared via depends_on fe57. The producer↔consumer schema/contract (8937) is explicitly scoped OUT and not required to stand up a behavioral round-trip. 822a binds to "the production path" ABSTRACTLY: the inbound comment/issuelinks/parent reads and outbound batch link/comment/parent dispatch its round-trip exercises already exist in production (the epic closes a test/probe-coverage seam, not re-fixes the bugs), and after 33d0's collapse the survivor IS the production path by construction — so no hard 33d0-before-822a edge is required (same abstract-binding pattern ruled benign for keystone 8937, applied symmetrically). No unmet "fix-must-land-first" edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 822a (BIDIRECTIONAL inbound↔outbound comment/link/parent ROUND-TRIP, test_roundtrip.py), 8937 (assembled snapshot-entry key/shape CONTRACT, test_snapshot_contract.py), 33d0 (OUTBOUND-only multi-sub-op consolidation correctness), 59ef (enrichment-METHOD return-shape fidelity) exercise the production path from DISTINCT angles on DISTINCT files — complementary, not duplicative. 822a explicitly scopes the schema/contract OUT (→8937).
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. 822a's edited files are disjoint from the 33d0↔2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster. The only hard ordering edge (fixtures before the consuming test) is captured by depends_on fe57.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. 822a AC3 keeps the hermetic round-trip in CI (pytestmark = integration) and any live variant opt-in/scheduled/documented (docs/jira-sync-setup.md), consistent with the epic's "live e2e never flakes CI" posture and parent "lean suite / no net increase in brittle tests"; AC4 asserts round-trip OUTCOMES, not whole-blob equality, imposing no brittle expectation on consumers.
- Residual references — BENIGN. The verify-inbound-comments-gap NOT-SYNCED guard 822a converts has been de-encoded (only an explanatory comment remains); no sibling deletes the probe scripts under scripts/jira-pressure-test, so its audit target was live and is now resolved. 33d0's deletion of the dead typed-leaf applier strands nothing 822a references — 822a drives the production batch path, not the typed leaf.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail criterion is a G3 coverage matter (and per the parent AC's verbatim "(owned by story B)" clause is owned by 8937) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 822a): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. Findings list EMPTY.
